sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see what happened:
1. Purple diamond moved from [41, 41] to [36, 42]
2. White diamond moved from [49, 40] to [40, 40]
3. Gray diamond at [33, 45] changed from G(12) to G(10) - it shrank!
So clicking on the purple diamond caused:
- The purple diamond to move up
- The white diamond to move up (from inside the orange outline)
- The gray diamond at [33, 45] to shrink from 12 to 10 pixels
This is interesting. The gray diamond shrank, which suggests that clicking on the purple diamond has some effect on nearby gray diamonds.
Let me think about this more carefully. The white diamond was at [49, 40] (inside the orange outline), and now it's at [40, 40]. The purple diamond was at [41, 41] and is now at [36, 42].
It seems like clicking on the purple diamond caused both the white diamond and purple diamond to move, and also affected the gray diamond at [33, 45].
Maybe the goal is to shrink all gray diamonds? Or to move the white and purple diamonds to specific positions?
Let me try clicking on the purple diamond again to see if it continues to move or if the gray diamonds continue to shrink.
